title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Cardinality Forensics
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a versioned read model and audited transition design and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug View Contracts
steps.instruction:
debug case — Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 102. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects.

Inspect the View Contracts trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug View Contracts.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy. For Inspect the failure record · Debug View Contracts, keep View Contracts connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: For View Contracts, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Trigger Audit Test
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-trigger-audit-test-6cl1qf" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; view, trigger, reusable-logic, or side-effect evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Trigger Audit Test.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Trigger Audit Test into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Trigger Audit Test, keep Trigger Audit Test connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-trigger-audit-test-6cl1qf
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id,
       CASE WHEN status = 'resolved' THEN resolved_at ELSE opened_at END AS relevant_time
FROM tickets ORDER BY relevant_time, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
